misconceptionSummary: Students multiply by 0.1 and get a larger answer, confusing it with multiplying by 10.

visuals.subText: Multiplying by one tenth is the same as dividing by ten

steps.narration: One tenth written as a decimal is zero point one. Multiplying by zero point one means taking one tenth of the number — which is the same as dividing by ten. Multiplying by zero point zero one means taking one hundredth — dividing by one hundred.
Forty-five times zero point one equals forty-five divided by ten. Dividing by ten shifts digits one place to the right: forty-five becomes four point five.
Forty-five times zero point zero one equals forty-five divided by one hundred. Dividing by one hundred shifts digits two places to the right: forty-five becomes zero point four five.

visuals.label: × 0.1 → ÷ 10 → shift right 1 place.  × 0.01 → ÷ 100 → shift right 2 places.

steps.narration: Four point five and zero point four five. Multiplying by zero point one or zero point zero one makes the number smaller — it is equivalent to dividing by ten or one hundred.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: 45 × 0.1 = 450 (treated 0.1 like 10). Multiplying by 0.1 is dividing by 10 — the answer gets smaller: 45 × 0.1 = 4.5.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Zero point one is one tenth — multiplying by it divides the number by ten. The result gets smaller, not larger. 45 × 0.1 = 4.5, not 450.
